# Day 10 — EF Core SQL Generation + Client Evaluation

## Original SQL Generated by EF Core

### Full Entity Query

```csharp
var fullQuotes = await context.Quotes
    .OrderBy(q => q.Id)
    .Take(5)
    .ToListAsync();
```

### SQL Generated

```sql
SELECT TOP(@p) [q].[Id], [q].[Author], [q].[CreatedByEmail], [q].[IsDeleted], [q].[Text]
FROM [Quotes] AS [q]
ORDER BY [q].[Id]
```

EF Core fetched every column from the Quotes table.

---

## Projected Query + Leaner SQL

### Projected DTO Query

```csharp
var projectedQuotes = await context.Quotes
    .Select(q => new
    {
        q.Id,
        q.Author
    })
    .OrderBy(q => q.Id)
    .Take(5)
    .ToListAsync();
```

### Leaner SQL Generated

```sql
SELECT TOP(@p) [q].[Id], [q].[Author]
FROM [Quotes] AS [q]
ORDER BY [q].[Id]
```

Using `.Select()` reduced the generated SQL to fetch only required columns.

---

## Client-Side Evaluation Caught and Fixed

### Problematic Query

```csharp
bool IsLongAuthor(string author)
{
    return author.Length > 10;
}

var clientEval = context.Quotes
    .AsEnumerable()
    .Where(q => IsLongAuthor(q.Author))
    .Take(5)
    .ToList();
```

### SQL Generated

```sql
SELECT [q].[Id], [q].[Author], [q].[CreatedByEmail], [q].[IsDeleted], [q].[Text]
FROM [Quotes] AS [q]
```

This query fetched all rows first and then filtered data in memory because `AsEnumerable()` forced client-side evaluation.

---

## Fixed Version

```csharp
var fixedQuery = await context.Quotes
    .Where(q => q.Author.Length > 10)
    .Take(5)
    .ToListAsync();
```

The filtering is now translated into SQL and executed directly on the database server.
